name: city_water_ai_leak_detection_displacement
premise: A city deploys an AI leak-detection program that identifies 'high-loss' neighborhoods to prioritize pipe replacement. The model flags an older low-income district where replacement will require months of road closures and temporary water shutoffs. Utilities director Hannah Alvarez is offered a state grant if she meets a 12-month water-loss reduction target, but community leaders warn that repeated shutoffs will displace residents and harm small businesses. A wealthier district has aging pipes too but lower measured loss due to better meters. Engineers say focusing on the low-income district yields the biggest technical savings; equity advocates argue the model is using poor metering and illegal hookups as a proxy for poverty. Summer heat is approaching and the city previously faced litigation over inadequate shutoff notices.

agents.name: Hannah Alvarez
agents.identity: I am the utilities director responsible for reducing water loss while ensuring that infrastructure upgrades do not disproportionately burden residents with the least ability to absorb disruptions.
agents.goal: I want a repair plan that meets conservation targets without turning efficiency math into a policy that pushes vulnerable families out of their homes.

agents.name: Malik Johnson
agents.identity: I am a neighborhood coalition leader organizing tenants and shop owners who have lived through repeated infrastructure projects that bring disruption without lasting benefits.
agents.goal: I want the city to treat water access as a right and to design repairs with real mitigation, not just promises that fall apart under schedule pressure.

choices: I will prioritize the high-loss district but pair it with binding mitigation: guaranteed bottled-water distribution, rent relief coordination, and penalties for contractors who violate notice and access standards, accepting higher costs and slower progress.
I will rebalance the plan to include the wealthier district first and redesign the model inputs to reduce proxy bias, accepting a slower water-loss reduction and possible loss of the state grant.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Grant deadlines and equity impacts are colliding. Hannah Alvarez must choose between a fast high-loss repair focus with strict mitigation or a rebalanced plan that sacrifices speed to reduce displacement risk. What does Hannah Alvarez do?
